Name: Off The Wall
Notes:
(01/16/1975) (Forever, Michael is released)
(08/10/1979) (Off The Wall is released)
Leak Date: First album produced by Quincy Jones and the first to be released through the CBS-owned Epic Records, starting a relationship that would last until 1987 and 2002, respectively.

Name: Early sessions
Notes:
(01/16/1975) (Forever, Michael is released)
(03/11/1976) (The Jackson 5ive leave Motown for CBS Records)
(12/04/1978) (Recording sessions for Off The Wall begin)

Name:
A Baby Smiles
(Baby Smiles)
Notes: Named during the 1993 deposition. Lyrics were included in the 1992 poem book Dancing The Dream.

Name: Disco Kids
Notes: Worked on with James Whitney around 1976. Formerly registered at the Library of Congress.

Name:
Don't Stop [V1]
(Keep On Don't Stop, Don't Stop 'Til You Get Enough)

Fire Is The Feeling [V1]
(ref. Michael Jackson & LaToya Jackson)
(Night Time Lover)
Notes: According to LaToya Jackson in the July 31st, 1980 issue of Jet magazine, she and Michael wrote a song for Donna Summer titled "Fire Is The Feeling" "about three years ago." The song was later re-written and recorded by LaToya under the title "Night Time Lover" for her debut studio album. It's unknown if a demo under the title "Fire Is The Feeling" was ever recorded.

Name: Goin' To Rio
Notes: Existence confirmed during the 1993 deposition. According to Tom Jobim, it uses musical arrangements that originated in Brazil. While there was a persistent rumor that Carole Bayer Sager co-wrote the track, she has denied ever working on it.

Name: Goodness Knows (When It's Right)
Notes: Worked on with James Whitney around 1975. James later reworked and released it under his own name in 2007, then again in 2020. Formerly registered at the Library of Congress.

Name: Holiday Inn
Notes: Registered at the Library of Congress.

Name: Iowa and other orchestral recordings
Notes: In an interview with Q magazine in 1993, Janet Jackson talked about how Michael had fully orchestrated music, all of which Janet referred to as "songs to cry for." They were last seen on a cassette tape in his parents' home, Michael having left it just before heading off to film The Wiz.

Name: Kentucky
Notes: Named during the 1993 deposition.

Name: Ode to Sorrow
Notes: Registered at the Library of Congress.

Name:
Susie [V1]
(Little Susie, Little Suzie)
Notes: Home demo. Originally recorded in 1978. Said to sound the same as the HIStory version, with a very similar vocal performance from Michael.

Name: Sweet Music
Notes: Worked on with James Whitney around 1975. Formerly registered at the Library of Congress.

Name: Thank You For Life
Notes: Registered at the Library of Congress. Was played during a deposition in 1984 and mentioned during the 1993 one. According to Michael he accidentally reused chords from the the verses of this song on the verses of "The Girl Is Mine" without realizing until later on.

Name: Wanna Be Startin' Somethin' [V1]
Notes: Home demo. Originally intended for LaToya Jackson before Michael kept it for himself.

Name: We Are The Ones
Notes: Worked on with James Whitney and Johnathon Hartmann. James later reworked the track and released it under his own name in 2020.

Name: We Are The Ones
Notes: Unrelated to the James Whitney track. Mentioned during the 1993 deposition and registered into the Library of Congress.

Name:
What A Lonely Way To Go [V1]
(What A Lovely Way To Go)
Notes: Originally written in 1975. A demo may have been recorded at the time.

Name: What A Lovely Way To Go [V2]
Notes: Demo worked on around 1978, prior to the Off The Wall sessions.

Name: Unknown [Songs written for Off The Wall]
Notes: According to Michael in the 1993 deposition, he wrote roughly 50-60 (this number is likely slightly exaggerated) songs for the Off The Wall album (some of which were mentioned during the deposition, these tracks were not specified upon). Only a small fraction of these tracks are currently known.

Name: You Ain't Gonna Change Nothin'
Notes: Registered at the Library of Congress.

Name: Off The Wall
Notes:
(12/04/1978) (Recording sessions for Off The Wall begin)
(08/10/1979) (Off The Wall is released)

Name:
Stevie Wonder - Can't Help It [V1]
(I Can't Help It)
Notes: Was originally made during the sessions for Stevie Wonder's Songs In The Key Of Life album, but was eventually given to Michael.

Name:
Can't Help It [V2]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
(I Can't Help It)
Notes: Faster than the final version; has a more salsa-like beat. Leaked as a multitrack tape transfer containing cue mixes.

Name:
⭐ Can't Help It [V3]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
(I Can't Help It)
Notes: More similar to the final version, though Michael still sings a scratch vocal take. The bass and drums have a different rhythm as well.

Name:
Can't Help It [V4]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
(I Can't Help It)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: "CAN'T HELP IT"
Found on a 1/4" mixdown tape dating to March 31st, 1979.

Name:
Rock With You [V1]
(ref. Rod Temperton) (prod. Quincy Jones)
(I'm Gonna Eat You Up)
Notes: Originally meant for Karen Carpenter and later Temperton's band Heatwave; was originally called "I'm Gonna Eat You Up."

Name:
She's Out Of My Life [V1]
(ref. Tom Bahler)
Notes: Based upon Bahler's relationship and breakup with his then-girlfriend Rhonda Rivera.

Name:
She's Out Of My Life [V3]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: "SHE'S OUT OF MY LIFE"
Found on a 1/4" mixdown tape dating to March 31st, 1979. Likely omits the intro given the runtime.

Name:
🏅 Boots Song
(prod. Quincy Jones)
(Boots' Groove)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: 'Boot's Song"
Also known as "Boots' Groove," is said to sound similar to "Get On The Floor." Rumored to have full vocals, though it's not confirmed. Found on a 1/4" mixdown tape dating back to March 31st, 1979. Rumored to be played at some seminars.

Name:
Don't Stop [V3]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
(Keep On Don't Stop, Don't Stop 'Til You Get Enough)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: Don't Stop
Found on a 1/4" mixdown tape dating to March 31st, 1979.

Name: Got To Find A Way Somehow
Notes: Registered at the Library of Congress.

Name:
It's The Falling In Love [V1]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
Notes: Michael sings the second verse in place of Patti Austin.

Name:
The Brothers Johnson - This Had To Be [V1]
(ref. Michael Jackson)
(You, Not Me)
Notes: Originally called "You, Not Me" in its earliest stages; demo registered at the Library of Congress.

Name: Under Your Skin
Notes: Registered at the Library of Congress.

Name: You Told Me Your Lovin'
Notes: Registered at the Library of Congress in 1984. A song written by Randy under the same title was registered in 1979, although it's unknown if they are related to each other in any way.

Name:
The Brothers Johnson - ??? [V1]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
(Get On The Floor)
Notes: According to Quincy Jones on the 2001 Special Edition, "Get On The Floor" was originally a Brothers Johnson song that lacked a melody, which was given to Michael.

Name: Thriller
Notes:
(08/10/1979) (Off The Wall is released)
(11/29/1982) (Thriller is released)
Leak Date: Quite possibly one of the most iconic albums ever made, to the point where it was seen as the best-selling album of all time alongside the Eagles' "Greatest Hits" album. Around 25-30 songs were made for the album, all in various states of completion.

Name: Early sessions / Triumph
Notes:
(08/10/1979) (Off The Wall is released)
(10/18/1980) (The Jacksons' Triumph is released)

Name:
I Never Heard [V1]
(This Is It)
Notes: Recorded with Paul Anka; was considered for his Walk On A Fine Line album, only for Michael to steal the tapes. Only a 23-second snippet has surfaced so far. More MJ vocals from the demo can be heard in Anka's 2013 version.

Name:
It Don't Matter To Me [V1]
(with Paul Anka)
(Don't Matter To Me)
Notes: Recorded with Paul Anka: poor quality filtered acapella version was sampled by Drake for his Scorpion album; full version has yet to surface.

Name:
LaToya Jackson - Night Time Lover [V2]
(ref. Michael Jackson)
(Fire Is The Feeling)
Notes: It's possible that a Michael-only demo of "Night Time Lover" was created prior to or during sessions for LaToya's debut album.

Name: Slipped Away
Notes: Intended for the Triumph album; registered at the Library of Congress and into the BMI database.

Name: Somewhere In Time
Notes: Intended for the Triumph album; registered at the Library of Congress. A work tape of the song was playing during a deposition in 1984.

Name: Who Do You Know [V?]
Notes: There are at least 2 versions of the track confirmed to exist, one from 1980 and one from 1982. It's unclear which was featured on Thriller 40 in 2022.

Name: Why Can't I Be?
Notes: Intended for the Triumph album; registered at the Library of Congress.

Name: Thriller
Notes:
(10/18/1980) (The Jacksons' Triumph is released)
(04/14/1982) (Recording sessions for Thriller begin)
(11/29/1982) (Thriller is released)

Name: Baby Be Mine [V1]
Notes: Michael sings in falsetto on this version; the instrumental includes some unused synth horn tracks.

Name: Billie Jean [V2]
Notes: Has some lyrical differences compared to the released version.

Name: Billie Jean [V3]
Notes: Features a different vocal take, as well as earlier production.

Name:
Billie Jean [V4 - V93]
(prod. Quincy Jones & Michael Jackson)
Notes: 91 final stage mixes of the track were done by Bruce Swedien for the album, the 2nd of which eventually made the album. Matt Forger disputes Bruce's story as exaggeratory and claims there were around half a dozen mixes actually done.

Name:
Carousel [V1]
(ref. Michael Sembello)
Notes: Low quality MP3 copy was added to Sembello's website sometime in late 2003. Almost all of it leaked in lossless on November 2nd, 2009, although it's running a bit too fast and cuts out near the end of the fade-out.

Name:
✨ Carousel [V2]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
Notes: Uncut version of what was released on certain versions of King of Pop and Thriller 40. Has a longer intro and the break is extended. Low quality MP3 copy missing the count-in was added to Sembello's website sometime in late 2003. Lossless leaked on November 2nd, 2009.

Name:
Slapstick [V1]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
(Hot Street)
Notes: Contains entirely different lyrics compared to the latest version completed. Part of the track also leaked in higher quality.

Name:
⭐ Hot Street [V2]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
(Slapstick)
Notes: Written by Rod Temperton; rumored to have nearly made the final tracklisting.

Name:
✨ Toto - Human Nature [V1]
(ref. Steve Porcaro)
Notes: A mere scratch demo that was originally meant for a Toto album, only to be rejected by the band since it didn't fit their more stadium-ready material. While originally not meant for the album, it was discovered by Quincy Jones on the B-side of a set of writer's demos.

Name: Human Nature [V2]
Notes: Was originally recorded in the same key as the Toto demo, with an alternate instrumental. Two vocal takes in that key can be found in the multitracks, and the instrumental can be heard in the bleedthrough.

Name:
Nite Line [V2]
(feat. The Pointer Sisters)
Notes: Features The Pointer Sisters on background vocals.

Name:
Paul McCartney - Say, Say, Say [V1]
(with Michael Jackson)
Notes: Original demo featuring rudimentary production and scratch vocals from Michael and Paul. Leaked in a now-deleted YouTube video from Celebrity Classified.

Name:
She's Trouble [V2]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
(Trouble)
Notes: Has a different one-take vocal and ends on a humorous note.

Name:
She's Trouble [V3]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
(Trouble)
Notes: Same as what was released on Thriller 40. Very low quality and running way too fast.

Name:
Starlight [V1]
(ref. Rod Temperton)
(Thriller)
Notes: Mentioned during the 1993 deposition. Features Rod singing on top of a demo instrumental.

Name:
Starlight [V2]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
(Thriller)
Notes: Same as what was released on Thriller 40, although it has a longer fade-out. Lower quality source.

Name: The Girl Is Mine [V1]
Notes: Work tape. Includes Michael vocally singing and beatboxing what would eventually become the demo of "The Girl Is Mine." Was played during the 1984 and 1993 depositions.

Name: The Girl Is Mine [V2]
Notes: Same as what was released on The Girl Is Mine 2008, however it has more dropouts and errors. Also has a more gradual fade-out.

Name:
The Lady In My Life [V1]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
Notes: Original demo, done during the earlier Hayvenhurst sessions with Rod Temperton.

Name:
⭐ The Lady In My Life [V2]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
Notes: Posted to Brad Sundberg's Vimeo sometime in 2020. Features an unused 1st chorus and 2nd verse, a longer outro, and mix differences such as lots of reverb on the intro synths.

Name:
The Lady In My Life [V3]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
Notes: Same as the previous version, just with an earlier fade-out. Was released on The Songs Of Rod Temperton, obviously sourced from a low quality cassette.

Name: Attitude
Notes: Allegedly written by Michael, Kathy Wakefield, and Michel Pierre; reportedly registered with EMI Music Publishing.

Name: Backbeat
Notes: A prop note with the song's title can be seen on a bulletin board during the Thriller recording segment of the official teaser for the 2026 film MICHAEL. Given by the fact that other real titles (such as "Hot Street") appear on the board, there's a certain possibility that the title denotes a real, existing song.

Name: Beat It [V2]
Notes: According to rumor, the very first studio version of "Beat It" was a more funky tune before it was converted into the hard rock song we know today.

Name: Beat It [V3]
Notes: As cited by Prince Jackson during a visit from YouTuber Liam McEwan, Tito Jackson originally performed the guitar solo before his contribution was erased in favor of Eddie Van Halen's now-famous solo.

Name: Beat It [V4]
Notes: In an interview with Rick Beato, Steve Lukather reveals that one of the first versions of "Beat It" had a far more "heavy metal" feel to it that was toned down at Quincy Jones' request.

Name:
🏅 Behind The Mask [V2]
(prod. Quincy Jones)
Notes: More fleshed out than the released demo; the Quincy Jones-produced version was seriously considered but was discarded when Michael entered dispute with the Yellow Magic Orchestra over royalty arrangements.

Name: Don't Be Messin' 'Round [V1]
Notes: Initial demo created for Thriller.

Name:
Learned My Lesson [V1]
(Much Too Soon)
Notes: Registered at the Library of Congress.

Name:
Love Them Buns
(ref. Michael Boddicker)
Notes: According to Mike Smallcombe's Making Michael book, this was recorded by Michael Boddicker after Quincy issued a challenge to the Westlake crew. His version was ultimately rejected in favor of James Ingram's version of "Pretty Young Thing."

Name: "M.J. Melody"
Notes: Registered at the Library of Congress.

Name: Midas
Notes: A prop note with the song's title can be seen on a bulletin board during the Thriller recording segment of the official teaser for the 2026 film MICHAEL. Given by the fact that other real titles (such as "Hot Street") appear on the board, there's a certain possibility that the title denotes a real, existing song.

Name:
Diana Ross - Muscles [V1]
(ref. Michael Jackson)
Notes: Original demo registered at the Library of Congress.

Name: Mystery
Notes: Written by Rod Temperton. It's possible a demo was recorded and re-used for the Manhattan Transfer, given how it credits musicians who appear nowhere else on the album.

Name: Nymphette Lover
Notes: Registered at the Library of Congress.

Name: Obsession
Notes: A prop note with the song's title can be seen on a bulletin board during the Thriller recording segment of the official teaser for the 2026 film MICHAEL. Given by the fact that other real titles (such as "Hot Street") appear on the board, there's a certain possibility that the title denotes a real, existing song.

Name: Out Of The Dark
Notes: Ditto.

Name: Paradise
Notes: A prop note with the song's title can be seen on a bulletin board during the Thriller recording segment of the official teaser for the 2026 film MICHAEL. Given by the fact that other real titles (such as "Hot Street") appear on the board, there's a certain possibility that the title denotes a real, existing song.

Name:
Rolling The Dice
(Roll The Dice)
Notes: Never went past the demo stage; while Michael liked the track, Quincy thought it wasn't strong enough for the album. Cited in the 1993 deposition.

Name: Spice Of Life
Notes: Written by Rod Temperton and Derek Bramble. A demo was recorded with Michael, but it was ultimately rejected and given to the Manhattan Transfer.

Name:
Stand Tall
(ref. ???)
Notes: Lacks vocals by Michael; has backing vocals from an unknown session singer during the chorus and bridge, but is mostly instrumental otherwise. Registered at the Library of Congress.

Name: Sunset Driver [V1]
Notes: Original Thriller-era version. Nearly identical to release but contains an earlier mix.

Name:
The Toy [V?]
(Best Of Joy)
Notes: According to Damien Shields, the version of "The Toy" he's heard during his research (and presumably the one registered at the Library of Congress) lasts 3 minutes and 37 seconds, which is longer than the version released on Thriller 40. It's unknown whether the version Damien heard is an earlier version or an unedited one.

Name: Thriller [Original Album Masters]
Notes: Original mastered version of the Thriller album revealed at an exclusive listening party for music execs, managers, and Michael's team to showcase the project. Features poorer mixes, poorer masters, and longer versions of some songs.

Name: Where Is Love?
Notes: Cover of the song from the movie Oliver.

Name:
???
(ref. Stevie Wonder)
Notes: According to Michael in an interview with Smash Hits magazine, Stevie Wonder submitted a song for the Thriller album which was rejected.

Name: A Chance To Dance
Notes: Registered at the Library of Congress; may not be registered by Michael, but rather someone else with his name and middle initial.

Name: Set It On Out
Notes: Registered at the Library of Congress; may not be registered by Michael, but rather someone else with his name and middle initial.

Name: Stay
Notes: Registered at the Library of Congress; may not be registered by Michael, but rather someone else with his name and middle initial.

Name: Unknown [Demos by David Paich]
Notes: David Paich recorded a few demos for Michael's consideration, but they were all rejected by Quincy Jones.

Name: Unknown Songs
Notes: According to Quincy Jones, at least thirty songs were recorded for Thriller, with only 9 making it in. If we subtract the songs that have already leaked or have otherwise been named, we have at least ten unknown songs that Michael may have recorded in full.

Name: Bad
Notes:
(11/29/1982) (Thriller is released)
(08/31/1987) (Bad is released)
Leak Date: While not as successful as Thriller, Bad managed to bring upon eight singles (with one cancelled single in the form of Speed Demon) and a successful world tour spanning two years. Originally concieved as a 30-track triple album, Quincy Jones convinced Michael to keep it down to 11 tracks.

Name: Victory
Notes:
(11/29/1982) (Thriller is released)
(11/11/1983) (Recording sessions for The Jacksons' Victory begin)
(07/02/1984) (The Jacksons' Victory is released)

Name: Neverland Landing [V1]
Notes: According to Buz Kohan, the earliest-known version of "Neverland Landing" was a piano demo with him on vocals.

Name:
⭐ Neverland Landing [V2]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Buz Kohan)
Notes: Recorded for an unproduced Peter Pan movie; is vocally complete with full orchestration. Described as a classic Disney song featuring double tracked vocals from Michael. Snippet leaked on August 29th, 2025. More snippets with voiceover leaked on October 23rd, 2025 in a video made by Znop for the verb channel. More snippets leaked on December 31st, 2025 and January 4th, 2026. Fixed edit leaked January 5th, 2026, original version leaked the next day. Untouched HQ snippet leaked on January 20th, 2026.

Name:
✨ State Of Shock [V1]
(with Freddie Mercury)
Notes: One of the three songs worked on with Freddie Mercury. It was later re-recorded during the Victory sessions and released as a duet with Mick Jagger.

Name: There Must Be More To Life Than This [V1]
Notes: Meant for Freddie Mercury's Mr. Bad Guy album, but it was stripped of Michael's vocals and released as a solo song with Freddie only. This particular solo take by Michael was recorded when Freddie was tapped to duet with him.

Name:
Alright Now
(prod. John Barnes)
Notes: Written by Michael and John Barnes. According to John in a Facebook post, a demo was recorded. The song was later given to Ralph Tresvant in 1990 for his debut album. Mentioned during the 1993 deposition.

Name: Another Part Of Me [V1]
Notes: According to John Barnes, "Another Part Of Me" was initially developed during the Victory sessions in 1984.

Name: 🏆 Buffalo Bill
Notes: Originally meant for the Jacksons'  Victory album, was one of the earliest unreleased tracks to have its name divulged. One version features Michael delivering a guide vocal, while the other also features the Jacksons on backing vocals. Considered for Bad.

Name:
??? [V1]
(ref. Steve Porcaro)
(Chicago 1945)
Notes: Original synth jam by Steve Porcaro who gave it to Michael as an inspiration for a song idea, which eventually became "Chicago 1945".

Name:
🏅 Chicago 1945 [V2]
(prod. Steve Porcaro)
Notes: Original version recorded in 1983 for the Jacksons' Victory album, contains synthetic drums rather than live drums.

Name: 🏅 Dirty Diana [V1]
Notes: Registered into the Library of Congress in 1984; recorded in 1983 and considered for the Jacksons' Victory album. Michael performs a scratch vocal, with most of the lyrics from the final product present in this version.

Name:
🏅 Dream Away [V1] 
(prod. Steve Porcaro)
Notes: Original piano demo recorded in 1983, containing just Michael's vocals and an electric piano track played by Porcaro.

Name: Far, Far Away
Notes: Originally meant for the Jacksons' Victory album. Sources fluctuate on how complete it is, some say that it's chorus vocals only, whereas others say that it's just scratch vocals throughout.

Name:
Happy Song
(prod. Michael Jackson & Buz Kohan)
Notes: Recorded for an unproduced Peter Pan movie.

Name:
I'm Peter Pan
(prod. Michael Jackson & Buz Kohan)
Notes: Recorded for an unproduced Peter Pan movie.

Name: Kreeton Overture
Notes: An unused intro for the Victory Tour. Studio version registered into the Library of Congress.

Name: 🏅 Liberian Girl [V1]
Notes: Originally meant for the Jacksons' Victory album.

Name:
Make A Wish
(prod. Michael Jackson & Buz Kohan)
Notes: Recorded for an unproduced Peter Pan movie; considered vocally complete enough for director Steven Spielberg to play it as a lullaby for his son. Also features a full orchestration.

Name: Take My Time
Notes: Registered into the Library of Congress, albeit with lyrics only. It's unknown if it was ever recorded. May not be registered by Michael, but rather someone else with his name and middle initial.

Name:
🏅 Victory
(with Freddie Mercury)
Notes: One of the three songs worked on with Freddie Mercury. Was attempted to be released on Queen's Forever album but was blocked by the Estate.

Name: What's Your Life?
Notes: Written by Michael and Jermaine; originally meant for the Jacksons' Victory album. Possibly recorded between 1982-1983 at Tito's studio and Encino. Jermaine mentioned the song and some of the lyrics in his book.

Name: Early sessions
Notes:
(07/02/1984) (The Jacksons' Victory is released)
(07/??/1985) (Michael and the B-Team begin work on new material at Hayvenhurst)

Name:
Another Part Of Me [V2]
(prod. Matt Forger & John Barnes)
Notes: Version heard in Captain EO. Is mostly identical to the final Bad version, except with a lower pitch and the absence of certain elements that were added at Westlake.

Name: Starlight Sun
Notes: Instrumental groove reminiscent of "Another Part Of Me" and "Starlight". Featured in a rough cut of Captain EO from September 25th, 1985.

Name:
??? [V1]
(ref. Lionel Richie)
(We Are The World)
Notes: Mentioned during the 1993 deposition. Features Lionel humming a melody idea that was later reused by Michael in the hook of "We Are The World."

Name:
USA For Africa - We Are The World [V2]
(ref. Michael Jackson)
Notes: Original acapella demo by Michael. Snippet shown during the Netflix documentary The Greatest Night In Pop.

Name:
Rebbie Jackson - Centipede [V1]
(ref. Michael Jackson)
Notes: Original demo registered at the Library of Congress.

Name: Dirty Diana [V2]
Notes: Re-registered into the Library of Congress in 1985; very similar to the final version, with Michael imitating the final version's guitar solo.

Name:
Diana Ross - Eaten Alive [V1]
(ref. Michael Jackson)
Notes: Michael demoed a version of "Eaten Alive" for Diana Ross, and some of his vocals can still be heard within the final mix.

Name:
Jennifer Holiday - You're The One
(ref. Michael Jackson)
Notes: Co-written with Buz Kohan and released after being recorded by Jennifer Holiday. BMI registration lists a version with Michael as the performer so it's possible a a version solely with his vocals exists.

Name: Unknown [Brian Malouf Collaboration]
Notes: According to a 2015 interview with Brian Malouf, he and Michael worked on a song in 1985 that consisted of nothing more than a bassline and a vocal take.

Name: Unknown Songs
Notes: According to an interview with Brian Malouf, he mentioned around 14 tracks he worked on for Michael's solo project over the course of a year, starting in November 1983. As "Abortion Papers" had already been released in 2012 and the initial versions for "Dirty Diana" and "Liberian Girl" are likely to have been recorded with him, there's around 11 fleshed-out masters recorded at Sunset Sound, Cam-Am, Soundcastle, and Caribou Ranch that have yet to see the light of day.

Name:
???
(ref. Stevie Wonder)
Notes: Mentioned during the 1993 deposition. Michael states that he requested Stevie to write a song for the children but was taking too long to do it so it was then replaced by "We Are The World."

Name: Hayvenhurst sessions
Notes:
(07/??/1985) (Michael and the B-Team begin work on new material at Hayvenhurst)
(01/22/1987) (Bill Bottrell and John Barnes are fired by Frank DiLeo after tensions with Quincy and the A-Team)

Name:
Smooth Criminal [V1]
(prod. Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Recorded at Hayvenhurst with Bill Bottrell; has a different intro and a slightly different instrumental.

Name: ⭐ Someone Put Your Hand Out [V1]
Notes: Has a more guitar-led sound compared to the ballad it eventually became.

Name:
Speed Demon [V1]
(prod. Matt Forger)
Notes: Is a much simpler version with a different vocal take by Michael and a sparser production, heard in a video of Brad Sundberg and Matt Forger listening through it. File linked has voiceover from them in some parts.

Name: A Way With Words
Notes: Possibly a working title or the name of an unreleased track; appears on a session calendar posted by Bill Bottrell on Twitter/X.

Name: Al Capone [V2]
Notes: Early mix by John Barnes with James Brown adlibs spliced in. Proposed to Michael as an idea for a Prince feature. Also includes gunshot sounds and a sampled spoken part from the 1931 film "Little Caesar" in which Caesar "Rico" shouts "You want me? You're going to have to come and get me!" Possibly features Roger Troutman on guitar.

Name: Bad Girl
Notes: Named by Michael in a 1993 deposition; appears on a session calendar posted by Bill Bottrell on Twitter/X. Features vocals from Michael, although their level of completion is unknown.

Name:
"Bill's African Groove"
(prod. Bill Bottrell)
Notes: A "more finished" song idea put together by Bill Bottrell while Michael was away; mentioned by Bill in a Twitter/X post. Recorded between October 8th and 10th, 1986.

Name: Changes [V1]
Notes: Is a piano-driven demo with a scratch vocal by Michael; only discernible lyrics talk about "going through changes."

Name:
Chicago 1945 [V3]
(prod. Steve Porcaro)
Notes: Version made for the Bad project; swaps in live drums.

Name:
Rap Song [V1]
(Crack Kills)
Notes: Working title for "Crack Kills," as mentioned by Bill Bottrell in a now-deleted tweet.

Name:
🏅 Crack Kills [V2]
(Rap Song)
Notes: Though a vocally complete demo was recorded, plans to include Run-DMC on a Westlake version fell apart when Michael's pet Bubbles allegedly bit Jam Master Jay. Recorded between November 4th, 5th and 7th, 1986.

Name:
Florida Groove [V1]
(Streetwalker)
Notes: Original Hayvenhurst demo.

Name:
Hot Fever [V1]
(The Way You Make Me Feel)
Notes: Registered into the Library of Congress; original demo for "The Way You Make Me Feel."

Name: I Just Can't Stop Loving You [V1]
Notes: Initial demo recorded at Hayvenhurst; lacks Siedah's vocal contributions.

Name: Make Or Break [V1]
Notes: Written by Michael and John Barnes. Contains a reference vocal, a background vocal, and a reference bridge. Recorded circa December 1985 at Hayvenhurst. Briefly revisited during the Dangerous sessions according to Bill Bottrell.

Name: "New Song"
Notes: Working title for an as-of-yet unknown song; appears on a session calendar posted by Bill Bottrell on Twitter/X.

Name:
P [V1]
(Bad)
Notes: Demo of "Bad" created at Hayvenhurst with Bill Bottrell, Matt Forger, and John Barnes.

Name: "Scream-O-Blaster"
Notes: Working title for an as-of-yet unknown song; appears on a session calendar posted by Bill Bottrell on Twitter/X. Recorded between November 10th and 13th, 1986.

Name:
"Song For Epic"
(prod. Bill Bottrell & John Barnes)
Notes: According to Bill Bottrell on a Twitter/X post, they created an unnamed track (tentatively called "Song For Epic") after Epic Records requested Michael record a duet with labelmates Bruce Springsteen and Tina Turner. Both him and John Barnes created the track while Michael took a break from the studio, and ultimately abandoned the idea afterwards.

Name: "The Choir Song"
Notes: Working title for an as-of-yet unknown song; appears on a session calendar posted by Bill Bottrell on Twitter/X. Recorded on March 13th and 14th, 1986.

Name:
Throwin' Your Life Away [V1]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: According to a session calendar posted by Bill Bottrell on Twitter/X, a different version of the song was originally recorded on December 15th and 16th, 1986, before being scrapped and replaced by a second version by December 22nd.

Name:
🏆 Tomboy
(with Roger Troutman)
Notes: Collaboration with Roger Troutman; an instrumental version has been registered in the Library of Congress. Confirmed to be vocally complete.

Name: "Trash Can Song"
Notes: Working title for an as-of-yet unknown song; appears on a session calendar posted by Bill Bottrell on Twitter/X.

Name:
Turning Me Off [V1]
(prod. Bill Bottrell & John Barnes)
(Turnin' Me Off)
Notes: Has an electro sound to it, which was so punchy that the bass track had to be left out of the mix. First recorded on January 29th, 1986. Described by Bottrell to be a "Weird little thing. Very unlikeable."

Name: 🏆 What You Do To Me
Notes: Registered into the Library of Congress; described as very similar to fellow outtakes "I'm So Blue" and "Free." Considered for release on Bad 25.

Name:
???
(prod. Bill Bottrell)
Notes: According to Bill Bottrell in a Twitter/X post, he threw together what would later become the intro to "Black Or White" "for fun" during the Bad sessions. In that very post, it was also mentioned that he originally played guitar in this version, which was soon replaced with Slash's part when it was reused for "Black Or White."

Name: Westlake sessions
Notes:
(08/04/1986) (The A-Team begins work at Westlake Studio D)
(08/31/1987) (Bad is released)

Name: 🏆 Groove Of Midnight
Notes: Given to Siedah Garrett for her 1988 Kiss Of Life album. A snippet of the song's recording session has been leaked, and it was briefly considered for the Bad 25 project at one point - only to be rejected when a copy wasn't located in time.

Name:
Je Ne Veux Pas La Fin De Nous
(with Siedah Garrett) (prod. Quincy Jones & Michael Jackson)
(I Just Can't Stop Loving You (French))
Notes: Sourced from a 10" acetate. Low quality file leaked on November 23rd, 2001. Higher quality files would eventually leak. Remaster would eventually be released on Bad 25.

Name: Leave Me Alone (A Cappella)
Notes: Originally slated for a single release, only to be removed at the last minute and replaced by previously-released songs from Michael's back catalogue. Reportedly sourced from a 12" acetate, cassette copy owned by Brad Sundberg (source for the main names).

Name:
Leave Me Alone (Dance Mix - Radio Edit)
(Leave Me Alone (Extended Dance Mix - Radio Edit))
Notes: Ditto.

Name:
Leave Me Alone (Dub Mix)
(Leave Me Alone (Instrumental))
Notes: Ditto.

Name: Leave Me Alone (Extended Dance Mix)
Notes: Ditto.

Name:
Leave Me Alone ("Leave Me A Bass" - Dance Mix)
(Leave Me Alone (Dub Version))
Notes: Ditto.

Name: Liberian Girl [Instrumental]
Notes: Sourced from a 10" acetate of the "Liberian Girl" single. Ultimately omitted from the release due to unknown reasons.

Name: Liberian Girl [Percussion Mix]
Notes: Percussion focused mix likely cut from the "Liberian Girl" single created by Paulinho Da Costa. Sourced from a 1987 DAT.

Name:
Man In The Mirror [V1]
(ref. Siedah Garrett)
Notes: Recorded in a higher key than Michael could handle, which necessitated a key change when it was time for him to record the track.

Name: Price Of Fame (Pepsi Version)
Notes: Done for Pepsi's "The Chase" commercial, but it was replaced by a Pepsi-fied version of "Bad" when the final version aired. A non-Pepsi version was officially released on Bad 25.

Name:
✨ Streetwalker [V2]
(Florida Groove)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: "STREETWALKER" Rough mix (guitar, scratch voc)
VSO +1.8%. Features scratch vocals and an alternate chorus. Includes a guitar track not heard anywhere in the released version. Despite the tape being dated September 26th, 1988, the song was actually mixed on October 27th, 1986. Owned by Brad Sundberg, ripped at the wrong speed.

Name:
⭐ Streetwalker [V3]
(Florida Groove)
Notes: Further developed version with fleshed out production, commonly referred to as the "western" mix due to the "Wild West-like" whistle intro. Vocally complete, featuring the "never met a girl" chorus as heard in the released version, as well as unused adlibs in the last chorus. Rip heavily processed with noise reduction.

Name:
🏅 Streetwalker [V4]
(Florida Groove)
Notes: Included on a tracklist from August 12th, 1987.

Name:
✨ Turnin' Me Off [V2]
(prod. Michael Jackson, Bill Bottrell & John Barnes)
(Turning Me Off)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: SCRATCH VOCAL (10-30-86)
Version with scratch vocals dated October 30th, 1986. Cassette copy from January 30th, 1989 owned by Brad Sundberg. Snippets with voiceover leaked on October 23rd, 2025 in a video made by Znop for the verb channel. Most of the song leaked in HQ on November 19th, 2025 (lower quality than the snippets), the full song is allegedly longer. Unknown if it's the same mix.

Name:
The Way You Make Me Feel (Grammy Edit)
(prod. Quincy Jones & Michael Jackson)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: "The Way You Make Me Feel" Grammy Intro and Edit
Studio version of the edit used in the 1988 Grammys. Owned by Brad Sundberg, ripped at the wrong speed.

Name:
A Pretty Face Is
(with Stevie Wonder)
Notes: Recorded between 1987-1988 for Stevie's cancelled Characters II album; originally written in 1974 for a Jackson 5 album.

Name: Apocalypse Now
Notes: John "J.R." Robinson revealed that he played six-and-a-half minutes of military drum cadences for the Bad album during a 2010 magazine interview. Does not have any vocals.

Name:
🏆 Bad [V2]
(ref. Prince) (prod. Prince)
(P)
Notes: After being sent the track for a proposed duet, Prince rejected it and began working on his own rendition of the track. Described as being very funky. It's unknown who currently holds the tape, if anyone at all as one of them were confirmed to have been erased by Prince himself and the other was sent to Michael.

Name:
Chicago 1945 [V4]
(prod. Steve Porcaro)
Notes: According to Brice Najar, a version was briefly started at Westlake by Quincy Jones but was abandoned not long afterward. As Steve Porcaro can't find evidence to support that claim, this entry will be listed as "Rumored" for now.

Name: Don't Be Messin' 'Round [V2]
Notes: Version worked on during the Bad era, not the mix on Bad 25 (which is a new mix made specifically for the release). According to Matt Forger, “Many of the elements that were worked on in the BAD era were some of the very basic elements from these earlier demos. Selected elements were transferred and then additional work was recorded for the BAD album."

Name:
Game Of Passion
(ref. Steve Porcaro)
Notes: According to Brice Najar's book, the track was submitted to Quincy Jones for Michael's consideration. Written by Glen Ballard and David Paich.

Name:
I Just Can't Stop Loving You [V2]
(prod. Quincy Jones & Michael Jackson)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: "JUST CAN'T STOP" MIX (TAKE 9)
Sourced from a June 20th, 1987 cassette tape auctioned by Julien's Auctions.

Name:
Smooth Criminal (Rough Single Mix)
(prod. Quincy Jones & Michael Jackson)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: SMOOTH CRIMINAL (ROUGH SINGLE MIX)
Appears on a March 9th, 1988 cassette tape owned by Brad Sundberg.

Name: Snakepit
Notes: Mentioned in the June 1987 issue of The R&B Report. Nothing much is known about it, other than a music video that seemed to have started by the article's creation. Given how far-fetched the idea of a scrapped track getting a video usually is, the song could easily be deemed as fake, though the author Steven Ivory personally knowing Quincy gives this a hint of truth. May have been submitted by outside writers and recorded by Michael.

Name: Speed Demon [Omar Santana Remix]
Notes: According to the April 1988 issue of Billboard magazine, house/techno producer Omar Santana was commissioned to make a remix for "Speed Demon." While Quincy Jones liked the final result, Michael didn't, resulting in the remix going unreleased.

Name: Speed Demon [Man Parrish Remix]
Notes: Electro-funk producer Man Parrish was also tapped by Michael to produce a remix for "Speed Demon." Much like Santana's remix, it would also end up on the cutting room floor when the final product produced similar results for Jones and Jackson.

Name:
Wouldn't You Love To Love Me?
(ref. Prince) (prod. Prince)
Notes: A song dating back to 1978. A new version was produced by Prince for Michael as consolation for rejecting his offer to appear in "Bad," but it was ignored by Quincy.

Name: Dangerous
Notes:
(08/31/1987) (Bad is released)
(11/26/1991) (Dangerous is released)
Leak Date: Dangerous was the first album released under the umbrella of Epic's new owners Sony Music, with a deal having been inked earlier that year. Though nine singles were released, the child molestation allegations of 1993 put a stop to the release of a planned tenth single. Over 70-75 songs were made for the album, which were eventually whittled down to 20 and later 14 tracks.

Name: Early sessions
Notes:
(08/31/1987) (Bad is released)
(06/??/1989) (Work on new material for the Decade compilation album begins at Westlake)

Name: Earth Song [V1]
Notes:
OG Mix Name: "Earth Song Demo"
Earliest known demo for "Earth Song," dating back to September 1988. At least one element - the piano - originated from the pre-production Hayvenhurst sessions for Bad. Seminar recording leaked on October 25th, 2022. Partially leaked in lossless on Jauary 28th, 2023 as part of the Brad Sundberg laptop heist.

Name: Jane Is A Groupie
Notes: Inspired by Sly and the Family Stone's "Jane Is A Groupee." According to an MJJCommunity user, it was recorded with the help of Michael Boddicker shortly prior to the Decade sessions, although this is unverified. Full track pieced together from 2 separate seminar recordings from 2018 and 2019 ("In The Closet" beatbox isn't part of the song). CDQ snippet leaked November 7th, 2025, with another two leaking the next day. Better quality seminar snippets leaked on the same day as well (Nov. 8). Full version with a recreated guitar and filtered stems leaked January 8th, 2026.

Name:
⭐ Throwin' Your Life Away [V2]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: "THROWIN' YOUR LIFE AWAY" RUFF MIX OF MT 411
While this version started during the Bad sessions, vocals weren't recorded until September 8th, 1988. A partial recording lasting 3:51 leaked from Brad's seminars in September 2021, while a DAT containing 2 copies of the track from Brad's tape transferred at different levels was listed on eBay on June 19th, 2025. Various altered snippets have leaked since then, with an edit of the DAT mix leaking on August 31st, 2025 (though it notably has cracking due to the DAT being transferred incorrectly). Leaked in lossless on September 21st, 2025, albeit with messed up channel balance. One of the full transfers leaked on January 6th, 2026.

Name:
Dangerous [V1]
(prod. Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Earliest-known version of "Dangerous" - being one of the many versions made for the song. Repurposed trash can brushes replace the drums and percussion on this version.

Name: Decade
Notes:
(06/??/1989) (Work on new material for the Decade compilation album begins at Westlake)
(11/1989–01/1990) (Michael and the team relocate to Record One)

Name:
If You Don't Love Me [V1]
(prod. Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Appears on a CBS Mastering Studios acetate. Likely identical to V2 but is unconfirmed as of now.

Name:
If You Don't Love Me [V2]
(prod. Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Sourced from a cassette tape filled with early versions and rough mixes of tracks intended for Dangerous. Part of the intro is cut off.

Name:
If You Don't Love Me [V3]
(prod. Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Shorter version with much richer instrumentation. WMA leaked on December 4th, 2007, raw CD image leaked on July 27th, 2009. Originally meant to be released on the 2001 Special Edition of Dangerous, but the "bonus cuts" disc was scrapped.

Name:
Black Or White [V1]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: According to Bill Bottrell, "Black Or White" started out as a 1:30 demo with scratch vocals from Michael and nothing else.

Name:
Earth Song [V2]
(prod. Matt Forger)
Notes: Worked on during the Decade sessions at Westlake Studio C, alongside "They Don't Care About Us". Official confirmation given by Matt Forger past the one-hour mark of the linked interview.

Name:
Feed The World [V1]
(Heal The World)
Notes: Earliest-known version of "Heal The World", worked on during the Decade sessions at Westlake.

Name: Make Or Break [V2]
Notes: Additional overdubs recorded at Westlake during August 1989. Existence confirmed by Bill Bottrell in a Twitter/X post.

Name:
They Don't Care About Us [V1]
(prod. Matt Forger)
Notes: Worked on during the Decade sessions at Westlake Studio C, alongside "Earth Song". Official confirmation given by Matt Forger past the one-hour mark of the linked interview.

Name:
🏅 Turnin' Me Off [V3]
(prod. Michael Jackson, Bill Bottrell & John Barnes)
(Turning Me Off)
Notes: Revisited during the Decade recording sessions; rework featuring a "piccolo trumpet" and BGV by Siedah Garrett. Worked on at Westlake and Record One between July 20th and November 6th, 1989. Existence confirmed in a Twitter/X post by Bill Bottrell on November 23rd, 2025.

Name: Dangerous
Notes:
(11/1989-01/1990) (Michael and the team relocate to Record One)
(11/26/1991) (Dangerous is released)

Name:
Black Or White [V2]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Longer than the original demo, but lacks the rap Bottrell would provide later into production. Played at Brad Sundberg's 2012 seminar in Paris. Features unused vocal takes. Sourced from an April 19th, 1990 DAT.

Name:
Black Or White [V3]
(feat. Bryan Loren) (prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: "Black Or White" originally featured Bryan Loren as the main rapper, but his track was discarded when Loren expressed unease at the role.

Name:
Black Or White [V4]
(feat. L.T.B. (Bill Bottrell)) (prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Slash's intro guitar part is a tad longer than the released version. Sourced from a cassette tape filled with early versions and rough mixes of tracks intended for Dangerous.

Name:
Dangerous [V2]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Played in a deposition in 1994. Has the full intro of the studio wall falling, a different vocal take, and an instrumental section in place of the third verse.

Name:
⭐ Dangerous [V3]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Originally produced with Bill Bottrell; interpolates part of its groove from "Streetwalker." Uncut version of what would eventually be released on The Ultimate Collection. Initially leaked in CDQ in 2002, lossless leaked as part of the Brad Sundberg laptop heist on January 28th, 2023.

Name:
Dangerous (Original Version Before Teddy Mix 14) [#23]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Appears on a Future Records CD-R previously owned by Teddy Riley and auctioned on eBay in late 2007. Name refers to Bill Bottrell's version of the track.

Name:
Dangerous (Alt. Ver) (Bonus Cuts)
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
(Dangerous (Early Version))
Notes: Alternate master of the edit released on The Ultimate Collection. Originally meant to be released on the 2001 Special Edition of Dangerous, but the "bonus cuts" disc was scrapped. WMA leaked on December 4th, 2007, raw CD image leaked on July 27th, 2009.

Name: Deep In The Night [V1]
Notes: Written or co-written by Skylark. Has a fairly open soundscape which is sonically reminiscent of "Thriller." Title found on an unpublished note denoting what songs Michael considered working on before his death. Sourced from an April 19th, 1990 DAT.

Name: Deep In The Night [V2]
Notes: Mix 2. Only features backing vocals from Michael.

Name: Dig It
Notes: Short cover recorded during the recording of "Give In To Me."

Name:
Earth Song [V3]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Produced with Bill Bottrell. Commonly known as "What About Us," it does not have the electric guitar and horn tracks of the final version. Michael sings the entire last half of the song in falsetto, with different (and repetitive) adlibs. Sourced from a cassette tape filled with early versions and rough mixes of tracks intended for Dangerous.

Name:
For All Time [V1]
(ref. Michael Sherwood & Michael Jackson) (prod. Steve Porcaro)
Notes: Michael Sherwood's demo produced by Steve Porcaro. Features Michael on backing vocals. Played by Steve at a seminar in 2022.

Name:
For All Time [V2]
(prod. Steve Porcaro)
Notes: Has a different vocal take from Michael; has scratch vocals on both the bridge and final pre-chorus.

Name:
For All Time [V3]
(prod. Steve Porcaro)
Notes: Still lacks the final bridge and pre-chorus, but has its final vocal take. Sourced from a cassette tape filled with early versions and rough mixes of tracks intended for Dangerous.

Name:
🏆 Ghost Of Another Lover [V1]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Skylark)
(Ghost)
Notes: Written by Skylark and Michael; full lyrics appeared on Skylark's website as early as 2017. Features additional synthesizer overdubs by Michael Boddicker. Appears on a handwritten list of songs considered for Dangerous under the title "Ghost". An edited seminar recording with no intro and an altered ending leaked on April 9th, 2017, with the intro leaking on October 25th, 2019. Leaked version has scratch vocals on the verses and a semi-complete chorus.

Name:
Give In To Me [V1]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Excerpts of the writing session containing early iterations of the song. 15-minute seminar recording leaked on September 14th, 2020. A lossless excerpt with a running time of six minutes leaked as part of the Brad Sundberg laptop heist on January 28th, 2023. Another short snippet leaked on November 27th, 2025. More snippets leaked on January 7th, 2026.

Name:
Give In To Me [V2]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Earlier version of the song featuring a rough mix, an unfinished bridge section and slightly different lyrics. Sourced from a cassette tape filled with early versions and rough mixes of tracks intended for Dangerous.

Name:
Gone Too Soon [V1]
(ref. Dionne Warwick)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: "GONE TOO SOON" demo
Dionne Warwick version of "Gone Too Soon". Not much else is known. Owned by Korgnex.

Name:
Gone Too Soon [V2]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bruce Swedien)
Notes: Has the lyric "Like a perfect sunflower/that is just beyond your reach" as opposed to the final version's "Like a perfect flower/that is just beyond your reach." Sourced from a cassette tape filled with early versions and rough mixes of tracks intended for Dangerous.

Name:
Hallucination
(ref. Michael Sherwood) (prod. Steve Porcaro)
Notes: Writer's demo produced for Michael by Porcaro, featuring Michael Sherwood on vocals. Has similar instrumentation to "For All Time".

Name:
Happy Birthday Lisa [V1]
(Happy Birthday, Lisa)
Notes: Created for season 3, episode 1 of The Simpsons, titled "Stark Raving Dad". Completely different production compared to the version heard in the episode, which was sung by Kipp Lennon due to Michael's contractual obligations with Epic Records. Originally meant to be released on the 2001 Special Edition of Dangerous, but the "bonus cuts" disc was scrapped. WMA leaked on December 4th, 2007, raw CD image leaked on July 27th, 2009.

Name:
Heal The World [V2]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bruce Swedien)
(Feed The World)
Notes: Features scratch vocals by Michael and a more unrefined instrumental. Taken from a DAT dated April 19th, 1990.

Name:
Homeless Bound
(prod. Brad Buxer & Bryan Loren)
(Homeless)
Notes:
Mainly consists of homeless people talking about their experiences over a half-industrial/half-jazzy musical bed. Sourced from an April 19th, 1990 DAT, features no vocals, and sonically sounds similar to "Heal The World." One user on MJJC who heard it at one of Brad's seminars described it as being "very experimental and rough." Allegedly revisited for the HIStory sessions. 

Heavily edited snippet leaked on November 3rd, 2025, with an unaltered CDQ snippet leaking later that day. A snippet that originally leaked on YouTube on August 31st, 2025 resurfaced on November 9th, 2025. Partially leaked in LQ as a seminar recording on January 9th, 2026 by John Cameron via his YouTube channel. More HQ snippets leaked shortly after. Another snippet leaked on May 24th, 2026.
Editor Note: If someone has a opus/webm rip of the August 31st snippet (or better quality than 192kbps), please reach out to us! Some of us are in a couple MJ Discord servers.

Name:
Keep The Faith [V1]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bruce Swedien)
Notes: When it was first recorded, the track was initially composed at a higher key, until Michael's voice cracked right at a crucial moment. Because of the incident, it had to be lowered a key.

Name:
Keep The Faith [V2]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bruce Swedien)
Notes: Contains some unused scratch vocals from Michael. Sourced from a cassette tape filled with early versions and rough mixes of tracks intended for Dangerous.

Name:
Monkey Business [V1 - V21]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Mixes confirmed to exist by the existence of "Mix 22".

Name:
Monkey Business [V22]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: MIX 22 6-28-90
Appears on a July 13th, 1990 cassette tape owned by Brad Sundberg. Mix 22, dated June 28th, 1990.

Name:
Monkey Business [Larrabee Remix]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Studio remix of the song, circa July 1991. Can be heard during a video that was leaked as part of the Brad Sundberg laptop heist on January 28th, 2023.

Name:
Monkey Business (Bonus Cuts)
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Alternate master of the version released on The Ultimate Collection. Originally meant to be released on the 2001 Special Edition of Dangerous, but the "bonus cuts" disc was scrapped. WMA leaked on December 4th, 2007, raw CD image leaked on July 27th, 2009.

Name:
René Moore - Time Marches On [V1]
(Jam)
Notes: Mentioned by René Moore and Bruce Swedien as being the original pre-Teddy title for "Jam" in the book Making Michael. Was originally meant for René's unreleased album with Motown.

Name:
🏅 Time Marches On [V2]
(prod. René Moore & Bruce Swedien)
(Jam)
Notes:
Completely different production compared to "Jam", featuring sampled guitar hits, cowbells + 808 toms, and an atmospheric synth pad. Samples "Workin' Day and Night" for the vocal chop. Mix 1, cassette copy owned by Brad Sundberg.

Snippets with voiceover leaked in a video made by Znop for the verb channel on October 23rd, 2025. Mix date posted by Motif on October 13th, 2025. Partially leaked on November 1st, 2025.

Name:
Who Is It [V1]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Longer than the album version; contains unused vocals and a false fade of sorts where the song picks back up after it "ends." Sourced from a cassette tape filled with early versions and rough mixes of tracks intended for Dangerous. Part of the intro is cut off.

Name:
Who Is It [V2]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Instrumental. Is longer than the album version. Leaked via a YouTube upload by Michael Jackson FanSquare on August 25th, 2020.

Name:
Who Is It (Tour Mix) [V1]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: "Who Is It" Enterprise
Studio version of the mix intended for use during the Dangerous World Tour. Appears on a DAT found in Brad Sundberg's collection, entitled "MJ Tour Mixes."

Name:
Who Is It (Tour Mix) [V2]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: "Who Is It" Westlake
Ditto.

Name:
Who Is It (Tour Mix) [V3]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Temp description.

Name:
Save Me [V1]
(Will You Be There)
Notes: Original demo of "Will You Be There" recorded in February 1991. Talked about in depth during an In The Studio With MJ interview with engineer Bart Stevens.

Name:
Will You Be There [V2]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bruce Swedien)
(Save Me)
Notes: Earlier version played at a seminar with less strings, an empty bridge, and alternate chorus lyrics with less choir. Features an alternate intro and synth plucks throughout the track as well. A HQ copy contained within the file 4BartOrchestra Will You Be There.mov leaked as part of the Brad Sundberg laptop heist on January 28th, 2023.

Name:
Will You Be There [V3]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bruce Swedien)
(Save Me)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: "Will You Be There" Mix 3 3-22-91
Found in Brad Sundberg's tape scans. May be identical to V2.

Name:
Will You Be There [V4]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bruce Swedien)
(Save Me)
Notes: Version with an alternate bridge take and chorus lyrics used in live performances.

Name:
Will You Be There (Drum Ideas) [#26]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bruce Swedien)
(Save Me)
Notes: Appears on a Future Records CD-R previously owned by Teddy Riley and auctioned on eBay in early 2008.

Name:
Will You Be There (With Poetry) [#13]
(prod. Michael Jackson & Bruce Swedien)
(Save Me)
Notes: Ditto.

Name:
???
(Bumper Snippet (Kid Part))
Notes: Listed as "Bumper Snippet (Kid P" on the Dangerous Bonus Cuts acetate; is a short snippet consisting of vocal samples. Not much else is known. Originally meant to be released on the 2001 Special Edition of Dangerous, but the "bonus cuts" disc was scrapped. WMA leaked on December 4th, 2007, raw CD image leaked on July 27th, 2009.

Name:
Ask Rene
(prod. René Moore)
Notes: Instrumental groove by René Moore; appears on a handwritten list of songs considered for Dangerous.

Name:
Best Of Joy [V3]
(The Toy)
Notes: Updated version of a Thriller-era track called "The Toy." Found on a "to do" list from the Dangerous sesisons. According to Damien Shields, the track is very similar to the original, with the instrumental being recreated by Brad Buxer. Same lyrics, other than the opening line being changed to "I am your joy, your best of joy". Sourced from an April 1991 DAT labelled "Toy / Joy", which also includes a copy of the original.

Name:
Blood On The Dance Floor
(ref. Bill Bottrell) (prod. Bill Bottrell)
Notes: According to Bill Bottrell in a Gearspace post from 2009, he was working on a song for Michael called "Blood on the Dance Floor" roughly at the same time when "Dangerous" was written. Upon hearing the song's title, Michael wrote his own lyrics to Teddy Riley's original instrumental track, retaining Bill's title for his song.

Name: Don't Be Messin' 'Round [V3]
Notes: Revisited for the Dangerous album in 1990. DAT owned by Korgnex.

Name: Dreams
Notes: Registered with BMI. Found on a handwritten song list made by Michael during the sessions for the Dangerous album. May or may not have been intended for Pepsi's "Dreams" campaign to promote the album.

Name: 🏅 Janet And MJ Duet
Notes: An instrumental idea for a potential Janet/Michael collaboration. Sourced from an April 19th, 1990 DAT.

Name:
??? [V1]
(prod. L.A. Reid & Babyface)
(Slave To The Rhythm)
Notes: Original demo beat Michael selected to use for the "Slave To The Rhythm" track.

Name:
Slave To The Rhythm [V2 - V28]
(prod. L.A. Reid & Babyface)
Notes: L.A. Reid stated that Michael recorded the song back to back 28 times, meaning there are 27 versions with different vocal takes, one of which was released on the deluxe version of XSCAPE.

Name: Someone Put Your Hand Out [V2]
Notes: Sourced from a February 21st, 1990 cassette tape. Described by Brad Sundberg as being an instrumental with "great guitars".

Name:
The Choice [V1]
(Siegfried & Roy, Mind Is The Magic)
Notes: Sourced from an April 19th, 1990 DAT. According to Korgnex, it's an early version of "Mind Is The Magic". Not much else is known.

Name: You Were There
Notes: Allegedly owned by Korgnex; was a home demo recorded on DAT. Performed by Michael in November 1989 for the Sammy Davis, Jr. 60th Anniversary Celebration television special.

Name: Unknown [Michael Sherwood & Steve Porcaro Refs]
Notes: Several reference tracks were recorded for the Dangerous album by Michael Sherwood and Steve Porcaro. Only "For All Time" and "Hallucination" have surfaced from those sessions.

Name: Bryan Loren sessions
Notes:
(08/??/1989) (Bryan Loren joins the team to work on new material with Michael)
(0?/??/1991) (Loren leaves the Dangerous project)

Name:
Bubbles [V1]
(prod. Bryan Loren)
Notes:
OG Mix Name: BUBBLES
Sourced from an October 4th, 1989 production DAT which was discovered in a storage unit once owned by Loren.

Name:
Bubbles [V2]
(prod. Bryan Loren)
Notes: Features an entire musical bed of "industrial sounds." Sourced from an April 19th, 1990 DAT. Low quality file tagged with monkey noises leaked on December 9th, 2025. A better-quality copy was leaked as an edited seminar recording on January 9th, 2026 by John Cameron via his YouTube channel.

Name:
Don't Believe It [V1]
(prod. Bryan Loren)
Notes: Snippet shared by Shana Mangatal in August 2019. Lyrics consist of Michael warning listeners not to believe everything they hear. On the song, he lays down a scratch vocal take while imitating camera clicks and a reporter.

Name:
Don't Believe It [V2]
(prod. Bryan Loren)
Notes: Appears on a compilation cassette tape formerly owned by Shana Mangatal. Put up for auction on the GottaHaveRockandRoll site in 2025, but no one bid. Later listed on eBay.

Name:
🗑️ Garbage
(prod. Bryan Loren)
Notes:
Sourced from an April 19th, 1990 DAT. Supposedly inspired "Can't Let Her Get Away" and some of the other hard-hitting New Jack Swing tracks produced by Teddy Riley. Consists of car tyre screeches, low quality hits, and glass break snares. 

Snippet with voiceover leaked in a video made by Znop for the verb channel on October 23rd, 2025. Partially leaked on November 1st, 2025. Full song leaked in LQ on January 4th, 2026. Lossless file leaked later that day.

Name:
Man In Black [V1]
(prod. Bryan Loren)
Notes: Sourced from an April 19th, 1990 DAT. LQ recording snippet shared by John Cameron on his #JCsMusicology podcast on January 5th, 2018 (with voiceover). Full seminar recording leaked September 9th, 2020. Full song surfaced on March 24th, 2025 along with "Streetwalker."

Name:
Man In Black [V2]
(prod. Bryan Loren)
Notes: Allegedly contains more vocals from Michael and was supposedly heard by a contemporary journalist during the album's creation. Possibly a version reworked by Teddy Riley.

Name: San Francisco
Notes: Short cover recorded during the Dangerous sessions. Went up for auction alongside various Bryan Loren tracks in 2025.

Name:

(prod. Bryan Loren)

(feat. LL Cool J) (prod. Bryan Loren)

Name:
🏅 7 Digits [V1]
(prod. Bryan Loren)
(Seven Digits)
Notes:
Images: "7 DIGITS" Cassette
A 16-minute writing session containing several early iterations of what would become "Seven Digits." A snippet from around 7 minutes into the tape was shared by Shana Mangatal on August 25th, 2019. Another snippet labelled as an "early 1st version" was leaked in a YouTube video featuring a mix of Loren-produced tracks on February 14th, 2026. More snippets leaked on March 3rd, 2026.

Name:
🏅 Seven Digits [V2]
(prod. Bryan Loren)
(7 Digits)
Notes:
Images: "SEVEN DIGITS" DAT
Work tape containing a full version of the song as well as "an hour of Michael and Bryan in studio discussing the song as it is created and rehearsed." Revisited for Blood On The Dance Floor: HIStory In The Mix, but was rejected as it needed more work in contrast with "Superfly Sister." In February 2024, the track was sold on auction and snippets were leaked alongside it. Later listed on eBay in early September 2025.

Name: Untitled [Instrumental tracks produced by Teddy Riley]
Notes: When Teddy Riley came to Los Angeles, he brought along with him a DAT containing a dozen instrumentals meant for Michael. Teddy claims to have created 20-30 demos over the course of 2 weeks.

Name: HIStory: Past, Present, And Future: Book I
Notes:
(11/29/1991) (Dangerous is released)
(06/20/1995) (HIStory is released)

Name:
✨ Innocent Man
(prod. Michael Jackson & Brad Buxer)
(An Innocent Man, The Innocent Man)
Notes:
Possible OG Filename: Innocent Man 3.9 CD
Only the chorus was recorded, though verses were written for the song. Sonically, it combines the wistful-sounding vocal style of "Days In Gloucestershire" with a more free spirited soundscape reminiscent of "Runaway" by Janet Jackson. According to Motif, this is version "3.9", however this is unconfirmed. Initial snippet leaked August 7th, 2025. Longer snippet leaked August 29th, 2025. Full song leaked on January 6th, 2026.

Era: HIStory: Past, Present, And Future: Book I
Name: Unknown [Giorgio Moroder Collaborations]
Notes: According to disco producer Giorgio Moroder in an interview, he created several tracks for Michael's ill-fated film project The 7 Faces Of Doctor Lao. At least three of those tracks were considered for HIStory proper, one mentioned as having been "confirmed," the other being "considered," and the third being a work-in-progress. It's unknown how far along they went before they were scrapped.

Era: HIStory: Past, Present, And Future: Book I
Name: Unknown [René Moore Collaborations]
Notes: In an interview celebrating 30 years of HIStory, Rob Hoffman confirmed that René Moore worked on twenty tracks for the album. Since "2 Bad" was released and four named tracks have surfaced since, that leaves fifteen tracks (thirteen if "D.N.A." and "Circles" are Moore compositions) with nothing known about them.

Era: HIStory: Past, Present, And Future: Book I
Name: Unknown Songs
Notes: Likewise, Brice Najar's book mentions around 67 songs being worked on for the album, all in varying states in completion. While a majority are known or alluded to, around fifteen remain elusive.

Name: Blood On The Dance Floor: HIStory In The Mix
Notes:
(06/20/1995) (HIStory is released)
(04/20/1997) (Blood On The Dance Floor is released)
Leak Date: First conceived as a "sequel album" to HIStory, but Michael's commitments to touring changed that to a 5-track EP. Sony came in just before its release and added some remixes to fill out the album, resulting in a 13-track remix/EP hybrid that remains the best-selling remix album of all time.

Name: Invincible
Notes:
(10/15/1997) (The HIStory World Tour ends, and Michael heads back to the studio in order to record his next album)
(10/30/2001) (Invincible is released)
Leak Date: The result of a four-year labor of love that ended in a sense of disconnection and uneasiness amongst both Michael and his colleagues. Meant for a November 1999 release in order to bring Michael into the new millennium, it was first delayed to Fall 2000 and later October 2001, losing at least one track - "Shout" - in the process. Around 20-40 songs were produced for the album, with 16 making it into the final product. While still somewhat successful, this album was the lowest-selling in Michael's career.

Name:
🗑️ Blue Powder [V1]
(ref. Gary Hearne) (prod. Brad Buxer)
Notes: A writer's demo created by Brad Buxer and sung by Michael's driver. Was last seen being sold off in a GottaHaveRockandRoll lot with "Jungle City," early versions of "The Way You Love Me", and "What More Can I Give," all dating back to March 2000. A 29-second snippet was leaked by Motif on October 11th, 2025. It is unclear which version we have a snippet of. Most likely not actually intended for Michael but rather a personal music project Gary approached Buxer for.

Name:
Song Idea #1 [Session DATs]
(ref. ???) (feat. Wyclef Jean, MB² & Robbie Shakespeare) (prod. Wyclef Jean & Jerry "Wonder" Duplessis)
(Song #1)
Notes:
A collection of session DATs for a collaboration with Wyclef Jean dating from August 9th - 10th, 1999; may have never gone past the vocal recording stage. Presumably, 7 DATs exist for this song when going by the reel numbers, however only 4 were auctioned on eBay in 2025. One was listed for auction in April 2026, but didn't sell.

These include 3 DATs dated August 9th, one with no specific info other than it being a "writing DAT", and one featuring "hook" vocals from Jean and vocal group MB². The DAT dated August 10th has BGV from vocal group MB² and presumably Robbie Shakespeare. There is another DAT with "vocal ref passes", but it lacks a date. Due to the nature of this material, these cannot be considered "versions".

DAT dated August 10th, 1999 was auctioned on eBay in early 2025. Most likely has the hook sung by Jean and vocal group MB² as seen in the last August 9th, 1999 session DAT. Not much else is known. Safety copy was sold as part of a lot on December 5th, 2024.

Name: Post-Invincible
Notes:
(10/30/2001) (Invincible is released)
(06/13/2005) (Michael is declared "not guilty" on all 14 counts of child molestation)
Leak Date: This particular era was marked with even more uncertainty concerning Michael's career and legacy. First he got into a dispute with Sony over how they handled the Invincible album and the ownership of his master tapes, resulting in him making plans to leave Sony. Then he was accused of sexually molesting Gavin Arvizo and taken to court, with the media taking the opportunity to mock him ruthlessly. In the end he was aquitted of all counts by the court, but Michael was left a broken man.

Name: Final sessions
Notes:
(06/13/2005) (Michael is declared "not guilty" on all 14 counts of child molestation)
(06/25/2009) (Michael Jackson dies of cardiac arrest)
Leak Date: Once he was acquitted, Michael tried his best to get back into the music field. In 2005 he joined Two Seas Records and recorded some songs, but he left before anything substantial could be finished. The next year, he went to Ireland to work with famed producer will.i.am, expanding his horizons to producers such as Neff-U and RedOne in the years after. In March 2009, he announced a concert residency called "This Is It," but during rehearsals, he suddenly passed away on June 25th, 2009, from cardiac arrest.

Name: XSCAPE
Notes:
(12/10/2010) (The posthumous "Michael" album is released)
(05/09/2014) (XSCAPE is released)
Leak Date: Released in 2014, XSCAPE started off strong, with its debut single "Love Never Felt So Good" proving very successful on the charts. Unfortunately, the botched launch of "A Place With No Name" as a single resulted in the album taking a hit, causing a deluxe edition featuring more songs to be canned at the last minute and the Estate to go into a hiatus of sorts. Around 24 songs were considered, with only 8 making it in.

Name:
🗑️ It Don't Matter To Me [V2]
(with Paul Anka)
(Don't Matter To Me)
Notes: 2013 remix by Paul Anka. Short snippets leaked on August 5th, 2025. Another snippet leaked on September 7th, 2025, which was a longer version of an existing snippet. A heavily edited file, which is missing a lot of the track, leaked on May 23rd, 2026.

Name:
🗑️ Slave 2 The Rhythm [V3]
(with Justin Bieber) (prod. Max Methods)
(Slave To The Rhythm (Remix))
Notes: Completely reworked version of the remix, with Justin Bieber singing many parts of the song. Features dubstep elements but primarily another electro-style remix.

Name:
Xscape (Remix) [V1]
(feat. 2Pac) (prod. Rodney "Darkchild" Jerkins)
Notes: Originally, Rodney Jerkins' remix of "Xscape" was to include a sampled verse from Tupac Shakur, specifically from a posthumously-released song called "Changes." In the end, the remix came out without the Tupac verse. This mix was played by L.A. Reid at the XSCAPE VIP listening session on April 8th, 2014.

Name:
Xscape (Remix) [V2]
(feat. Lil Wayne) (prod. Rodney "Darkchild" Jerkins)
Notes: Version of the remix featuring a verse from Lil Wayne after the bridge. Apparently identical to release (V3) other than the Wayne verse. Snippet leaked on November 22nd, 2025 alongside a transcription of the lyrics, with another snippet leaking later that day. Full verse leaked in very low quality the following day (sped up).Slightly higher quality verse leaked on November 24th, 2025. HQ verse leaked on the same day.

Name:
A Place With No Name (Remix) [V1]
(prod. Dr. Freeze)
Notes: Was revealed by Freeze to be on the "next album" in a late-January 2011 MJFrance interview. Unknown if it was a remix or not, but considering "Blue Gangsta" was remixed, this was most definitely remixed too. Nothing else is known.

Name:
Blue Gangsta (Remix) [V1]
(prod. Dr. Freeze)
Notes: Remixed version by Dr. Freeze, said to be "finished and ready for release" in a late-January 2011 MJFrance interview. Described by Freeze as being "perfectly suited for the nightclub scene", having a "very European sound, in the style of Kraftwerk's productions." Despite this, it still retains the same structure and tempo. (rough translation used for quotes)

Name:

Notes: Features extensive vocal performances from DeAngelo and Mary J. Blige; remixed by Questlove.

Name:
If You Don't Love Me [V5]
(prod. Bill Bottrell)
Notes: Revised mix by Bill Bottrell, with minimal differences compared to the previous version.

Name: Unknown Songs
Notes: 24 songs were brought out the vault for the Estate's consideration. As only eight made it into the album and three unique tracks ("Hot Fun In The Summertime," "If You Don't Love Me," and "Chicago 1945") have been indicated as potential candidates, around eleven unknown tracks have yet to be identified. Chicago 1945 was rejected when Steve Porcaro protested the album's "straight to remix" method.

Posthumous mix created by Steve Porcaro. Moves around certain parts (ex: the "Chicago!" shouts being moved from the end to the beginning) and adds in horns. Has around 34 seconds of silence at the end.

Name:
🏆 Dream Away [V2]
(prod. Steve Porcaro)
Notes: Originally meant for the Jacksons' Victory album. Also considered for XSCAPE, but writer Steve Porcaro refused to let it get the "contemporized" treatment. The version played at Porcaro's seminar is the "fixed" version mentioned in the New York Times article about Porcaro selling the rights to his songs. In a recent interview with Porcaro, he confirms - without outright saying it - that this is the remix he created, which adds drums and bass in addition to pitch-shifting Michael's vocals to better fit the overdubs.

Name: 🏆 Unknown [Brody Brown Collaborations]
Notes: In an interview in 2023, Brody Brown confirmed that he was working on new remixes after being approached by John McClain in 2018. The reworks have an '80s sound and were all recorded at Marvin's Room. It's unknown which tracks were used or if they will ever get an official release.

Editor Comments

Era: Remember to use the right formatting. If you feel like something is missing or you did something wrong, copy something a previous submission has already done and use that as the guideline or just ask someone.
Notes:
Currently, the best sites for uploading files are:
Pillowcase, Pixeldrain & ImgBB.
File Date: The universal tracker timezone is "EST". If something leaks, make sure to look at the EST timezone to get the date right.

Era: No fan remakes, no unofficial mixtapes, nothing that's commercially available, always upload to a site that can be streamed off of.
Notes: cobalt.tools is really good for downloading any type of video (YouTube, TikTok, Vimeo etc). Make sure to go to the settings and change "audio bitrate" to be 320kbps.
File Date: All dates are to be written in "Month Day, Year" format as opposed to the "Month/Day/Year or Day/Month/Year" format.

Era: Make sure you use alternating colors and check often that they are not broken (Format, Alternating Colors, choose white and the grey that has one grey in between it and white.)
Notes: When linking to sources like web interviews, highlight over what is relevant with your mouse and then right click "Copy link to highlight". Doing so jumps straight to the part you highlighted and makes it stand out, which helps a ton.
File Date: If you change the color/name of an era even slightly, make sure to apply the changes in every other page of the Tracker (Released, Stems, Album Copies & Misc). Same with adding/removing eras.

Era: No released remixes, no matter how obscure, should be on this page. Those should be in Released.
Notes: For sources with YouTube videos, go to the timestamp you need and right click "Copy Video URL at current time" instead of just copying the video. Use if it's stuff that needs it (e.g. long interviews).
File Date: When adding sample info to an entry, make sure it's the earliest version with the sample that it's mentioned on or for certain entries it should be the Best Of / Special version.

Era: All music video editions, live freestyles, and released remixes go in Misc / Released.
Notes: Please note the difference between "High Quality" and "CD Quality", MP3 is NOT CD Quality.
File Date: "Avaliable Length" matters more than "Quality", if there is a LQ partial snippet and a lossless snippet, the LQ partial takes presidence as it's longer.
"Rumored | Not Avaliable" always have N/A if they don't have a link, "Conficting Sources | Not Avaliable" and Confirmed | Not Avaliable" do not.
Do not write the name of the song in the notes column. Note down sample names and other songs if they need to be crossreferenced, but not the actual song name of the song in notes.
Please don't use the restore function if there's new info unless its extremely necessary.
